items.prompt: Describe the rigid transformation that maps one figure onto the other on a coordinate grid.
items.expected_evidence: show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Classify the triangle and justify the classification using its defining attributes.
items.expected_evidence: compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find a missing angle or side measure using the structure of the diagram.
items.expected_evidence: use spatial language to describe clockwise and counterclockwise turning situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates use spatial language to describe clockwise and counterclockwise turning situations with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain which properties remain unchanged under a rigid transformation and why that supports congruence.
items.expected_evidence: show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams.

items.prompt: Explain why the figure belongs to a category in the shape hierarchy.
items.expected_evidence: compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ.

items.prompt: A student claims two figures are congruent because they look the same size by eye. Diagnose the justification gap. Focus on ray length bias.
items.expected_evidence: show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams.

items.prompt: A student reflects a point across the x-axis but changes both coordinates. Identify the transformation error. Focus on turn direction confusion.
items.expected_evidence: compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ.

items.prompt: Use a transformation or coordinate argument to justify whether two figures are congruent in a less-familiar diagram.
items.expected_evidence: show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Apply angle or triangle reasoning in a contextual or composite-figure setting.
items.expected_evidence: compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves compare two angles by amount of turn even when side lengths differ in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, describe a rigid motion on a grid and explain which measurements are preserved.
items.expected_evidence: show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates show quarter-turn, half-turn, and full-turn actions and match them to diagrams on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Check position direction and spatial language first so the system can distinguish an upstream spatial-structure gap from a true angle concept and turn measure misunderstanding.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether position direction and spatial language is secure enough to support angle concept and turn measure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in position direction and spatial language from a current-node issue in angle concept and turn measure.

items.prompt: Check polygon attributes and hierarchy first so the system can distinguish an upstream spatial-structure gap from a true angle concept and turn measure misunderstanding.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether polygon attributes and hierarchy is secure enough to support angle concept and turn measure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in polygon attributes and hierarchy from a current-node issue in angle concept and turn measure.
